ttl.dbg.connect: Connection settings, translated to Tera Term command-line options. Omit to let the macro connect itself.
ttl.dbg.connect.host: Host name or address (ssh / telnet), or named pipe path (namedpipe).
ttl.dbg.connect.port: TCP port number (ssh / telnet). Sent as host:port. (/P=)
ttl.dbg.connect.speed: Speed (baud rate) of the serial port (serial). (/BAUD=)
ttl.dbg.connect.cdatabit: Data bits of the serial port: 7 or 8 (serial). (/CDATABIT=)
ttl.dbg.connect.cstopbit: Stop bits of the serial port: 1, 1.5 or 2 (serial). (/CSTOPBIT=)
ttl.dbg.connect.cflowctrl: Flow control of the serial port (serial). (/CFLOWCTRL=)
ttl.dbg.connect.cdelayperchar: Transmission delay per character of the serial port, in ms (serial). (/CDELAYPERCHAR=)
ttl.dbg.connect.cdelayperline: Transmission delay per line of the serial port, in ms (serial). (/CDELAYPERLINE=)
ttl.dbg.connect.waitcom: When the serial port does not exist, wait for the connection (serial). (/WAITCOM)
ttl.dbg.connect.options: Extra raw Tera Term options appended as-is, e.g. ["/auth=password", "/user=admin"].
ttl.dbg.logFile: Start Tera Term with logging to this file. (/L=)
ttl.dbg.hidden: Start Tera Term in hidden (invisible) state. (/V)
ttl.dbg.multicastName: Multicast name for the sendmulticast macro command. (/MN=)
ttl.dbg.osc52: Operations to permit for clipboard access from the remote host. (/OSC52=)
ttl.dbg.autoWinClose: Automatically close the window on disconnection. (/AUTOWINCLOSE=on|off)
ttl.dbg.disableLocalEcho: Disable the TCPLocalEcho and TCPCRSend options. (/E)
ttl.dbg.newConnectionDialog: Show (true, /ES) or hide (false, /DS) the 'New connection' dialog on startup. Note: usually suppressed when a macro is given.
ttl.dbg.teraTermDir: Directory containing ttermpro.exe. Auto-detected (e.g. C:\Program Files\teraterm5) when empty.
ttl.dbg.launcher: Launcher executable. 'ttermpro' passes the macro via /M= to ttermpro.exe (default). 'ttpmacro' launches ttpmacro.exe and injects the connect command via a temporary wrapper macro. 'ttpmacro-attach' attaches to an already-running ttermpro.exe session via ttpmacro.exe /D=.
ttl.dbg.launcher.ttpmacro: ttpmacro.exe — connection injected via wrapper macro
ttl.dbg.launcher.ttpmacro-attach: ttpmacro.exe /D= — attach to an existing ttermpro.exe window
ttl.command.formatWorkspace: Format all TTL files in workspace
ttl.config.format.normalizeOperatorSpaci: Normalize whitespace around operators and commas on format (e.g. 'a=1' becomes 'a = 1'). Strings and comments are preserved; unary minus and command-argument negatives (e.g. 'mpause -1') are left untouched.
ttl.config.format.alignCommentTables: Align markdown tables written inside comments on format, accounting for full-width characters.
ttl.config.format.maxConsecutiveBlankLin: Maximum number of consecutive blank lines kept on format. Set to 0 to disable collapsing.
ttl.config.language: Language for hover documentation. 'auto' uses the VS Code display language.
ttl.config.language.auto: Detect language from VS Code UI setting
ttl.config.maxNestingDepth: Maximum allowed block nesting depth before a warning is reported. Set to 0 to disable the check.
ttl.config.includeRootDir: Base directory for resolving include paths on include navigation. Empty = auto-detect the top-level parent macro by scanning the workspace. A relative path is resolved against the workspace folder; an absolute path is used as-is.
ttl.config.requireLabelInSameFile: Require goto/call labels to be defined in the same file. When enabled, labels found only in included files are NOT accepted: go-to-definition (F12) searches the current file only, and references without a same-file definition are reported as errors (the error diagnostic also requires ttl.diagnostics.undefinedLabel to be enabled).
ttl.config.diagnostics.undefinedLabel: Warn when goto/call references a label with no matching definition (includes are resolved). Suppressed automatically when an included file cannot be resolved.
ttl.config.diagnostics.unknownCommand: Warn when a line starts with an unknown command, suggesting the closest known command.
ttl.config.diagnostics.duplicateLabel: Warn when the same label is defined more than once.
ttl.config.diagnostics.includeNotFound: Warn when an included file cannot be resolved (does not exist). Offers a quick fix to replace it with a similarly named existing .ttl.
